body:
As far as I can tell, [this comment](https://github.com/z3z1ma/target-bigquery/blob/9e9121a/target_bigquery/core.py#L581) is inaccurate because the `BEGIN TRANSACTION` / `COMMIT TRANSACTION` [syntax](https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/transactions#transaction_scope) are not used, and they can't be used in this case either because [transactions don't support creating or dropping non-temporary tables](https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/transactions#statements_supported_in_transactions). 

`CREATE OR REPLACE` command is an alternative which makes the table replacement atomic, making sure there's no gap in the middle where the target table is unavailable. Separating the temp table drop into a separate query ensures we only delete the temp table if the target table was successfully overwritten.
title: use CREATE OR REPLACE instead of separate DROP IF EXISTS + CREATE TABLE

body:
### Summary

- Introduces configurable column selection for MERGE via merge_column_strategy.
- Extracts MERGE SQL assembly into `_build_merge_statement(...)` for clarity/testability.
- Documents the new behavior and configuration in README.md.

### Motivation
MERGE operations could fail when source and target schemas diverge. This adds resilient strategies to avoid referencing non-existent columns and optionally evolve the target schema additively before MERGE.

### What’s changed
 - `target_bigquery/core.py`
     - New helper: `_build_merge_statement(bigquery_client, target, source_table, using_table_sql) -> str`.
     - `merge_table(...)` now delegates column strategy and SQL construction to the helper.
     - Supports three strategies:
       - `target_all` (default): use all target columns (legacy behavior).
       - `intersection`: use only columns present in both source and target.
       - `alter_then_intersection`: add additive columns from source to target, then MERGE using intersection.
- `target_bigquery/target.py`
     - New config option: merge_column_strategy with enum values target_all, intersection, alter_then_intersection.
- `README.md`
     - Added docs for merge_column_strategy and its behavior.

### Configuration

- Default: target_all (backward compatible).
- Recommended for resilience: intersection.
- For immediate column propagation: alter_then_intersection (requires additive DDL perms).

### Backward compatibility
- Default behavior unchanged (target_all).
- No breaking changes.

### Testing
Manual validation:
- `target_all`: unchanged behavior.
- `intersection`: MERGE succeeds when source misses target-only columns.
- `alter_then_intersection`: new source columns added to target and included in MERGE.

### Risks
- `alter_then_intersection` performs DDL (additive only). Requires BigQuery permissions.
- Frequent additive schema updates on large tables may add minor overhead.
title: feat(core): add merge_column_strategy for resilient MERGE

body:
As of Singer SDK [`v0.31.0`](https://github.com/meltano/sdk/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#v0310-2023-08-07), record validation now checks key properties. In combination with #116, when using any of the `column_name_transforms.*` settings, this causes a validation error. I can make the change in #116 instead if you prefer, but I believe it is better off as its own change as it likely also affects how `upsert` works currently.

Probably goes without saying, but this is with `denormalized: true`.
title: fix: Apply column name transformations to key properties

body:
Closes #111

Python 3.8 support was removed in [`v0.43.0`](https://github.com/meltano/sdk/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#v0430-2024-12-10), so I wasn't able to update beyond that (out of scope for this PR).
title: feat: Update SDK

body:
Bumps [orjson](https://github.com/ijl/orjson) from 3.8.10 to 3.9.1.
Release notes

Sourced from orjson's releases.

3.9.1

Fixed

  • Fix memory leak on chained tracebacks of exceptions raised in default. This was introduced in 3.8.12.

3.9.0

Added

  • orjson.Fragment includes already-serialized JSON in a document.

3.8.14

Changed

  • PyPI manylinux wheels are compiled for x86-64 instead of x86-64-v2.

3.8.13

Changed

  • Source distribution contains all source code required for an offline build.
  • PyPI macOS wheels use a MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET of 10.15 instead of 11.
  • Build uses maturin v1.

3.8.12

Changed

  • Exceptions raised in default are now chained as the __cause__ attribute on orjson.JSONEncodeError.

3.8.11

Changed

  • orjson.loads() on an empty document has a specific error message.
  • PyPI manylinux_2_28_x86_64 wheels are compiled for x86-64-v2.
  • PyPI macOS wheels are only universal2 and compiled for x86-64-v2 and apple-m1.
Changelog

Sourced from orjson's changelog.

3.9.1 - 2023-06-09

Fixed

  • Fix memory leak on chained tracebacks of exceptions raised in default. This was introduced in 3.8.12.

3.9.0 - 2023-06-01

Added

  • orjson.Fragment includes already-serialized JSON in a document.

3.8.14 - 2023-05-25

Changed

  • PyPI manylinux wheels are compiled for x86-64 instead of x86-64-v2.

3.8.13 - 2023-05-23

Changed

  • Source distribution contains all source code required for an offline build.
  • PyPI macOS wheels use a MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET of 10.15 instead of 11.
  • Build uses maturin v1.

3.8.12 - 2023-05-07

Changed

  • Exceptions raised in default are now chained as the __cause__ attribute on orjson.JSONEncodeError.

3.8.11 - 2023-04-27

Changed

  • orjson.loads() on an empty document has a specific error message.
  • PyPI manylinux_2_28_x86_64 wheels are compiled for x86-64-v2.
  • PyPI macOS wheels are only universal2 and compiled for x86-64-v2 and apple-m1.
Commits

  • 4550226 Py_DECREF for owned traceback and type objects
  • 18b6562 Set permissions for stale
  • e15a5b6 3.9.0
  • e2180eb cargo update, rustup
  • dcab26b orjson.Fragment
  • 96522e8 More serializer objects, size
  • cd4330a 3.8.14
  • f02d38f manylinux x86-64 instead of x86-64-v2
  • 399c591 lint misc
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

New Contributors

Sourced from packaging's changelog.

23.1 - 2023-04-12


* Parse raw metadata (:issue:`671`)
* Import underlying parser functions as an underscored variable (:issue:`663`)
* Improve error for local version label with unsupported operators (:issue:`675`)
* Add dedicated error for specifiers with incorrect `.*` suffix

* Relax typing of ``_key`` on ``_BaseVersion`` (:issue:`669`)

body:
Bumps [simplejson](https://github.com/simplejson/simplejson) from 3.18.4 to 3.19.1.
Release notes

Sourced from simplejson's releases.

v3.19.1

Version 3.19.1 released 2023-04-06

  • This release contains security hardening measures based on recommendations by a security audit sponsored by OSTIF and conducted by X41 D-Sec GmbH. Several of these measures include changing defaults to be more strict, by default simplejson will now only consume and produce compliant JSON, but the flags still exist for any backwards compatibility needs. No high priority issues were discovered, the reference count leak is thought to be unreachable since the digits of the float are checked before PyOS_string_to_double is called. A link to the public version of this report will be included in a future release of simplejson. The following fixes were implemented in one PR: simplejson/simplejson#313
  • Fix invalid handling of unicode escape sequences in the pure Python implementation of the decoder (SJ-PT-23-01)
  • Fix missing reference count decrease if PyOS_string_to_double raises an exception in Python 2.x; was probably unreachable (SJ-PT-23-02)
  • Backport the integer string length limitation from Python 3.11 to limit quadratic number parsing (SJ-PT-23-03)
  • Fix inconsistencies with error messages between the C and Python implementations (SJ-PT-23-100)
  • Remove unused unichr import from encoder (SJ-PT-23-101)
  • Remove unused namedtuple_as_object and tuple_as_array arguments from simplejson.load (SJ-PT-23-102)
  • Remove vestigial _one_shot code from iterencode (SJ-PT-23-103)
  • Change default of allow_nan from True to False and add allow_nan to decoder (SJ-PT-23-107)

v3.19.0

NOTE: This release was not published to PyPI as the version number was mistakenly not updated in setup.py

Version 3.19.0 released 2023-04-06

  • This release contains security hardening measures based on recommendations by a security audit sponsored by OSTIF and conducted by X41 D-Sec GmbH.   • Fix missing reference count decrease if PyOS_string_to_double raises an exception in Python 2.x; was probably unreachable (SJ-PT-23-02)
  • Backport the integer string length limitation from Python 3.11 to

... (truncated)

Several of these measures include changing defaults to be more strict, by default simplejson will now only consume and produce compliant JSON, but the flags still exist for any backwards compatibility needs. You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. title: Bump simplejson from 3.18.4 to 3.19.1

body:
Bumps [actions/setup-python](https://github.com/actions/setup-python) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/setup-python's releases.

v4.0.0

What's Changed

  • Support for python-version-file input: #336

Example of usage:

- uses: actions/setup-python@v4
  with:
    python-version-file: '.python-version' # Read python version from a file
- run: python my_script.py

There is no default python version for this setup-python major version, the action requires to specify either python-version input or python-version-file input. If the python-version input is not specified the action will try to read required version from file from python-version-file input.

  • Use pypyX.Y for PyPy python-version input: #349

Example of usage:

- uses: actions/setup-python@v4
  with:
    python-version: 'pypy3.9' # pypy-X.Y kept for backward compatibility
- run: python my_script.py

  • RUNNER_TOOL_CACHE environment variable is equal AGENT_TOOLSDIRECTORY: #338

  • Bugfix: create missing pypyX.Y symlinks: #347

  • PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable: #400

  • Added python-path output: #405 python-path output contains Python executable path.

  • Updated zeit/ncc to vercel/ncc package: #393

  • Bugfix: fixed output for prerelease version of poetry: #409

  • Made pythonLocation environment variable consistent for Python and PyPy: #418

  • Bugfix for 3.x-dev syntax: #417

  • Other improvements: #318 #396 #384 #387 #388

Commits
  • d27e3f3 Fix outdated ref to actions/setup-python@v3 in doc (#581)
  • dec86ec Bump json5 from 2.2.0 to 2.2.3 (#579)
  • 4dcd676 Update CODEOWNERS (#576)
  • 8b89ef0 Use correct Poetry config when collecting Poetry projects (#447)
  • 5ccb29d Install multiple python versions (#567)
  • c3e0339 Update action to use reusable workflows (#569)
  • 206e984 refactor: Use early return pattern to avoid nested conditions (#566)
  • 2c3dd9e Add OS info to the error message (#559)
  • 76bbdfa Update minimatch (#558)
  • 1aafadc Caching projects that use setup.py (#549)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://dependabot-badges.githubapp.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=actions/setup-python&package-manager=github_actions&previous-version=3&new-version=4)](https://docs.github.com/en/github/managing-security-vulnerabilities/about-dependabot-security-updates#about-compatibility-scores) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself)
